content:
I have a theory.

The things that make your first job memorable are rarely the things in your job description.

They're the awkward conversations.

The tiny wins.

The mistakes you never make twice.

And the moments that quietly make you better.

I turned some of those lessons into this carousel.

I'd love to know...

What's something your first job taught you that no one ever warned you about?
